message:
fix(tui): use char count instead of byte length for message prefix width calculation

- Fix message list rendering in TUI where multi-byte UTF-8 characters
  in the prefix (marker, badge, ID, timestamp, project badge) caused
  incorrect remaining-width calculation, leading to premature subject
  truncation. Switch from .len() (byte count) to .chars().count()
  (character count) for accurate terminal column accounting.
- Update Cargo.lock with crossbeam-epoch and crossbeam-deque
  dependency additions for fsqlite-mvcc and fsqlite-vdbe crates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2331,6 +2331,7 @@ name = "fsqlite-mvcc"
 version = "0.1.0"
 dependencies = [
  "blake3",
+ "crossbeam-epoch",
  "fsqlite-error",
  "fsqlite-observability",
  "fsqlite-pager",
@@ -2403,6 +2404,7 @@ dependencies = [
 name = "fsqlite-vdbe"
 version = "0.1.0"
 dependencies = [
+ "crossbeam-deque",
  "fsqlite-ast",
  "fsqlite-btree",
  "fsqlite-error",
